hero.question: Will Houston Astros win the 2026 American League Championship Series?
hero.prob_take: At 6.7%, the market is pricing the Astros as dead money in the AL playoffs, which feels harsh for a franchise that's won 100+ games in 3 of the last 5 years and still has Altuve and their rotation intact. The real question is whether Houston's division strength (always facing tough competition) is baked into this, or if a healthy October run at 15-to-1 odds represents value against a field of 13 other AL contenders.

daily_take.headline: Astros ALCS Collapse Signals Smart Money Knows Something
daily_take.deck: Houston's championship odds just tanked 7.5 points to 6.7%, the sharpest single-day move in weeks on $683K volume. The market is pricing in either a rotation implosion or front-office dysfunction that Vegas hasn't caught yet. If you disagree with the pessimism, the value lives on the other side of this panic.

sidebar.headline: Steyer's California momentum sticks around, but 12.6% still looks underpriced for a billionaire willing to spend eight figures on name recognition.
sidebar.label: Tom Steyer Governor: 12.6%

sidebar.headline: Mavericks money keeps flowing in as the market reprices their title odds upward, suggesting sharp syndicates smell a rotation that can actually coexist on offense.
sidebar.label: Mavericks vs. Bucks: 31.5%

sidebar.headline: Citi takes SpaceX public at 87.5%, but microscopic $2K volume means you're trading against ghosts, not smart money. Light on conviction.
sidebar.label: Citigroup SpaceX IPO: 87.5%
